free_text: Cross section measurements of the reactions induced by
deuteron particles on 13C
2 MV Tandetron accelerator
The thin 13C foil used for cross section measurements
was provided by ACF metals. The thickness was
Three PIPS (passivated implanted planar silicon)
detectors (700 mu-m  depleted zone) were used for the
detection of charged particles emitted by nuclear
reactions. The detectors were respectively positioned
at 135., 150. and 165. degr. with respect to the
incident beam.
The absolute uncertainty on the cross
sections. It takes into account the uncertainties on
the measurement of:
* the 13C foil composition (2.5%),
* the areal density of gold (2.0%),
* the number of emitted particles originating from
the nuclear reactions under study (<3%) and
* the number of deuteron backscattered by gold
nuclei (<0.5%).
Data received from Dr. Julien L.Colaux
Entry was approved by Dr. Julien L.Colaux
